source: The scoring bible (books/neko-death-cult/scoring.md) + the assembler/mixer (tools/assemble.py, tools/mix.py), battle-tested across the 24-episode audio drama.
signature: industrial-goth cyberpunk engine — deep distorted 808 sub-bass, pounding industrial kick, cold detuned analog synths, cyberpunk arpeggios, strobe-hum drone, vinyl/VHS grit, neon reverb, and a ghost of Fosse muted trumpet. ~70 BPM. One haunting romantic motif that swells then dissolves.

generator.rule: Everything under the spoken word is INSTRUMENTAL — narration is never fought by sung lyrics. The door's song (album track) becomes the episode theme only in instrumental form.
generator.tos_gotcha: Music generators REJECT prompts that NAME real artists (bad_prompt). Describe era/sound/instrumentation only — never the artist. (TTS is unaffected; names are fine in narration.)

theme.name: Signal Finds Signal
theme.role: series main theme — open and close of every episode; the album title track (#24)
theme.prompt: instrumental main-title theme for a dark cyberpunk audio drama; industrial-goth engine — deep distorted 808 sub-bass, pounding industrial kick, cold detuned analog synths, cyberpunk arpeggios, strobe-hum drone, vinyl/VHS grit, neon reverb, a ghost of Fosse muted trumpet; one haunting romantic motif that builds to a full cinematic swell then drops to a loopable bed; ~70 BPM; instrumental, no vocals

logo_sting.role: series sound-logo (~8 s) under the identifier voice
logo_sting.prompt: short cinematic sound-logo, dark cyberpunk-goth; a paper-tear whoosh into a deep 808 hit and a rising neon-synth swell, vinyl crackle, a single distant beckoning-cat bell; ominous, branded, ~8 seconds; instrumental, no vocals
logo_sting.motif: the single distant beckoning-cat (maneki-neko) bell is the audio mnemonic

scene_beds.vault: early-2000s industrial-goth nightclub heard through walls (EBM/cyber-goth); four-on-the-floor kick, distorted 808, cold synth stabs, strobe-hum, fog hiss; hypnotic, menacing, loopable, mixed low
scene_beds.cathedral: cavernous glitching techno-gospel; reverberant choir pads, distant broken church organ, dial-up static, granular glitches, deep sub drones; sacred and broken
scene_beds.motel: cold minimal dread; one detuned drone, sparse sub pulses, fluorescent hum, faint clock tick, vast empty reverb; affectless, bureaucratic, menacing by restraint
scene_beds.europa: warm but uncanny morning ambience; soft Rhodes, analog pads, distant birdsong/cutlery, a faint detune under the warmth so it feels too clean; tender and wrong
scene_beds.cosmic: cosmic lofi; dreamy downtempo, warped tape, celestial pads, a slow jukebox arpeggio, deep 808 heartbeat, vinyl crackle, starfield reverb; awe and dread
scene_beds.outro: end-credits reprise of the main theme; the romantic motif swelling then dissolving into a long reverb tail; unresolved, cinematic; fades out
scene_beds.underbed: very quiet subliminal dark-ambient drone; almost inaudible floor of tension under narration; no melody, no drums

episode_anatomy: OPEN (~25-30s): logo sting -> main theme swell -> series identifier VO ducked over it -> swell into story
COLD OPEN: theme drops to a low bed, story begins
SCORED NARRATION: scene-specific instrumental beds under the voice, ducked ~-16 dB, swelling at beats
STING TRANSITIONS: needle-lift / paper-door SFX at scene breaks
OUTRO: theme reprise + whispered mantra, fading out

mix_chain.voice_bus: narration stays fully intelligible; TTS via the SPZ voice kit (see voices.json)
mix_chain.bed_fades: afade in/out per bed; loop bed to scene length
mix_chain.crossfades: acrossfade d=4 between sections; c=tri for bed-to-bed, c=qsin for open->body
mix_chain.limiter: alimiter=limit=0.97 on the master (0.97 true-peak ceiling)
mix_chain.open_build_note: FFmpeg 8.1: build each open stage (sting head, theme bed, delayed VO) as separate single-output mp3 files, then amix RAW inputs and acrossfade — the terminal -map '[label]' path mis-maps and the mp3 muxer rejects multi-stream output.

sample_the_show.concept: the show's own voice (SPZ) is sampled INTO the album songs — clean intro/mid, haunting-faded outro
sample_the_show.isolate: demucs --two-stems=vocals on the song; ffmpeg silencedetect=noise=-30dB:d=1.5 on vocals.wav to find pockets
sample_the_show.haunt_filter: asetrate=45000 (pitch down) + aecho reverb + volume=2.5 + afade out
sample_the_show.layer: adelay per sample, volume~2.3, amix inputs=N:duration=first:normalize=0, alimiter=limit=0.97
